title: Backend Manager (Supply Chain & Customer Success) — US Dropshipping Store Part-time to start ? Full-time as we scale
description:
Must haves: 

Ownership & accountability — takes end-to-end responsibility; drives results and SLAs.



Process discipline (SOP-driven) — follows steps exactly; documents and ensures consistency.



Problem-solving & proactivity — spots risks early (tracking, OOS, address) and acts before escalation.



High-impact communication — concise, clear, and timely with suppliers, CS, and management.



Prioritization & time management — works from checklists/deadlines 



Experience with tools: Shopify, parcel pannel, supplier groupchat on whatsapp, gmail, chat gpt, disputifier.



Stress tolerance — stays calm during delays, peak volumes, and chargeback risk.



Nice-to-have competencies



Negotiation & persistence — keeps suppliers to SLAs; friendly but firm.



Continuous improvement (Kaizen mindset) — finds process gaps and closes feedback loops.



Systems thinking — understands upstream/downstream impact (listings ? fulfillment ? CS ? refunds).



Leadership/coaching — can lead and QA the CS team as we scale.



Tool fluency — Shopify, Parcel Panel, Stripe/Shopify Payments, Sheets (filters/exports).



Risk control & dispute prevention — minimizes complaints/chargebacks with tight comms.



Flexible availability (US hours) — adapts when customer/supplier timing requires it.





About the Role



We’re a fast-growing fashion e-commerce brand selling to the USA. We’re hiring a Backend Manager who will own the supply chain and order health end-to-end. You’ll start part-time and handle customer service alongside backend tasks; as we scale, you’ll transition to full-time focusing on supplier communication, order issue resolution, and leading the CS team.



Key Responsibilities



Supplier Contact Protocol



Follow up when:



Tracking Number Missing (not uploaded in Shopify).



Tracking Not Updating (no scans for 3 days).



Supplier Address Errors (we have full address; supplier flags issue).



Product Material Info Needed (request specs/material details).



Push supplier for faster processing when delays occur; require response within 24 hours.



Dispute Prevention



If tracking doesn’t work: send order # to supplier and notify customer we’re checking.



Update customer immediately upon supplier response.



After delivery, follow up to confirm receipt if there were issues.



Order Issues (Decision Tree)



Address Error ? ask CS to
 customer to confirm/correct address.



Variant OOS ? request similar product (1080×1080 images) from DayOne; instruct lister to remove the OOS variant and replace with approved images/options.



Size OOS ? ask customer for alternative size (always advise bigger size). If no larger size: refund. Remove OOS size from store and follow the issue-handling timeline.



Product OOS ? get similar product (1080×1080), instruct to set current product to Draft and replace assets/options accordingly.



Unfulfilled with No Update ? ask DayOne in the Unfulfilled Orders channel for reason and next steps; escalate to Joel if unclear.



Parcel Panel Oversight



Daily check Parcel Panel in Shopify.



Export orders in “Info Received” for >5 days since purchase; post order numbers + store name in Supply Chain channel and tag Cheny (DayOne) to prioritize shipments.



Refund Workflow



SCM tracks supplier refunds; CS processes customer refunds in the store and leaves them as open issues in the sheet until supplier refund is confirmed.



Issue-Handling Timeline (Monitor & Enforce)



Day 1: Order in with issue ? contact customer.



Day 2: 1st
 sent.



Day 5: 2nd
 (reminder).



Day 8: No response ? refund customer.



Refused/Uncollected parcels: If returned to sender, refund order minus €15 handling.



SLAs & Reporting



Never leave an order unfulfilled >7 days; coordinate with CS/Bastian to refund if exceeded.



After every refund of unfulfilled orders, confirm in Supply Chain chat.



Daily update in Supply Chain channel confirming all stores checked + highlight blockers.



Customer Service (initially)



Handle tickets related to shipping status, address confirmation, OOS options, and post-delivery follow-ups.



Maintain professional, on-brand communication for US customers.



Team Enablement



Send clear instructions to listers to remove OOS variants/sizes/products and upload replacement images.



As we scale, lead and QA the CS team (SOP compliance, tone, response times).



Required Experience (Must-Have)



2+ years in e-commerce backend / supply chain / order operations for a dropshipping store selling to the US market (exact field experience required).



Daily hands-on with Shopify Admin, Shopify Payments/Stripe, Parcel Panel, and supplier portals.



Proven experience preventing disputes/chargebacks via proactive tracking, comms, and SLAs.



Strong English (written & spoken), concise documentation, and SOP adherence.



Time-management across multiple stores; comfortable with daily checklists and KPIs.



Nice to Have



Experience working with DayOne or similar fulfillment partners.



Basic spreadsheet automation (filters/exports) and clear loom/text updates.



Familiarity with Shop Pay, chargeback portals, and address verification flows.



Schedule



Part-time start (overlap with US business hours required).



Availability to move to full-time as order volume scales.



KPIs You’ll Own



% orders unfulfilled >7 days (target: 0%).



% tracking “no update >3 days” resolved within 24h.



Supplier response time (?24h).



Refund timeliness (Day 8 rule).



CS SLA & first response time (during initial phase).



Tools



Shopify, Shopify Payments/Stripe, Parcel Panel, Google Sheets/Excel, Slack (or equivalent),
 helpdesk (e.g., Gmail/Helpdesk).



Compensation



Competitive; commensurate with experience.



Part-time initially; path to full-time plus role growth (owning supply chain & CS leadership).



How to Apply



Begin your first line with: Strawberry.



Share a short note (?150 words) on a time you prevented chargebacks with proactive tracking/comms.



Attach or link to your CV and two references from e-commerce stores you supported.



Confirm:



Your years of dropshipping backend experience (US market).



Your tools proficiency (Shopify, Parcel Panel, Stripe).



Your weekly availability now and willingness to move to full-time.

title: Backend Manager fashion dropship (high scale)
description:
THIS JOB STARS AS PART TIME AND CAN GROW INTO PART TIME AS WE SCALE. ABSOLUTE EXCELLENCE IS NEEDED FOR THIS POSITION - ONLY A-PLAYERS. PLEASE DON'T REPLY TO THIS IF U ARE NOT AN A-PLAYER AND DO NOT MEET THE REQUIREMENTS. IF U THINK U ARE A PERFECT FIT, SEND A MESSAGE TO
ON WHATSAPP WITH A SHORT APPLICATION. 



???? Backend Manager / Right-Hand Operator — Fashion E-Commerce (Full-Time, Remote)

Position: Full-Time Backend Manager (Right Hand)

Hours: 40 hours/week (weekdays + weekends)

Starting Pay: $5.00/hour + performance bonuses

Pay Schedule: Bi-weekly

Industry: Fashion E-Commerce / Dropshipping



About Us

We run a high-volume fashion dropshipping operation targeting the Australian market, with suppliers based in Asia. We're currently processing 50+ orders per day and generating serious daily revenue — and we're scaling. Our model moves fast, pivots when needed, and rewards sharp thinking.

We're not looking for someone to fill a seat. We're looking for an A-player — someone who takes genuine ownership, sees problems before they become fires, and helps this business grow beyond where it is today. If that's not you, this isn't for you.



The Role

You will be my right hand — the person who keeps the backend of this business running flawlessly so I can focus entirely on the frontend. You're not just executing tasks; you're thinking ahead, protecting margins, managing relationships, and setting the standard for everyone else on the team.

This is a role for someone who works because they want to — not because they were asked to.



What You'll Be Responsible For



Order Management: Review all orders daily for address issues, sizing problems, and data errors before they cause refunds, disputes, or chargebacks.

Supplier Communication: Maintain tight, professional daily contact with our Asia-based supplier. Ensure all orders are fulfilled on time, every time. Request and verify invoices daily.

Quality Control: Request and review quality checks for fashion products to keep our refund and chargeback rates as low as possible. You understand that a low chargeback and refund rate is a key KPI — and you take it personally.

Customer Service Oversight: Review the customer service team's work once per day. Check response times, tone, and accuracy. Flag anything that's off and correct it immediately.

SOP Adherence: Follow our Standard Operating Procedures without deviation. You don't take shortcuts.

Supply Chain Management: Keep a firm grip on the full supply chain — from order placement to delivery. Identify and resolve bottlenecks proactively.

Chargeback & Dispute Management: Use Disputifier to fight chargebacks and win. You know the process, you know the evidence required, and you don't let illegitimate disputes slide.

Staff Management & Hiring: Manage current tea
mbers, hold them accountable, and recognize when performance is slipping. When growth demands it — or when someone is clearly coasting — you identify the need for a new hire and take initiative. You understand which roles a scaling fashion dropship business needs: ads listers, product listers, social media managers, supply chain managers, customer service reps, invoice checkers, and more — and you know when to bring them in.

Product Listings & Size Charts: Experience with product listings and Kiwi size charts is required.

Reporting & Admin: Maintain clean, accurate Excel sheets for tracking performance, invoices, orders, and team output.

AI-Assisted Workflows: Actively use ChatGPT, Gemini, or other AI tools to improve efficiency and quality of work.

Initial CS Support: In the early stages, you'll also jump in on customer service
s when needed — no task is beneath you.





What We Need From You



Minimum 5 years of experience as a Backend Manager

Minimum 2 years of experience specifically with fashion dropshipping stores

Proven experience managing high-volume order stores (50+ orders/day)

Deep understanding of the dropshipping business model — including market switches, rebuilding phases, and scaling periods

Proficiency with: Shopify, Gmail, Excel, Disputifier

Experience with Kiwi size charts and fashion-specific quality control

Experience with supply chain management in a fashion context

Fluent in written and spoken English — no exceptions

Must be proficient with AI tools (ChatGPT, Gemini, or similar)

Comfortable working across Australian and Asian time zones

Available 40 hours per week, including weekends

Currently not employed elsewhere — this is a strict requirement. This role demands full focus and availability. If you are working another job, please do not apply.





Who You Are



You are organized, fast, and thorough — mistakes cost real money here and you know it

You are stress-resistant — when volume spikes or something breaks, you don't panic, you solve

You are proactive — you spot issues before they're issues, and you bring solutions, not just problems

You push me to be better — you'll call out what needs to improve and back it up with data or reasoning

You bring fresh ideas and real improvements to the operation

You are a fast learner who picks up new tools, workflows, and tasks without being hand-held

You work on your own initiative — not waiting to be asked

You understand that in this business, every error costs money and every good decision earns it





Compensation



Starting rate: $5.00/hour

Performance bonuses tied to chargeback and refund rate benchmarks — stay below the targets, earn more

Salary increases as you grow with the business and demonstrate consistent results

Payment: Bi-weekly (alternative arrangements can be discussed if needed)





A Note on Fit

We're doing $7,000/day in revenue. This isn't a hobby operation. Every piece of this role matters — the invoices, the quality checks, the disputes, the team management, the supplier calls. If you're the person who quietly makes everything run better, who doesn't need to be micromanaged, and who takes real pride in a clean, efficient backend — you'll thrive here.

Somewhere in this job post is a small test. If you read every single word carefully — which any serious candidate should — you will know exactly how to begin your application (With the word ''Strawberry''). We use this to filter out mass-applicants from people who actually mean business.



How to Apply

Send your application with:



A brief introduction about yourself and your background

Specific examples of fashion dropshipping stores you've managed

Your experience with Disputifier, chargebacks, and quality control

Your current availability and timezone

Any tools, systems, or improvements you've introduced at previous roles



If you do not meet all of the requirements listed above, please do not apply.

We look forward to hearing from the right person.

title: **AMAZING Media Buyer (Facebook Ads Specialist) Needed ASAP***
description:
We are an innovative marketing company headquartered in New York, known for accelerating growth for DTC brands through strategic solutions and data-driven optimizations. We are seeking an experienced Media Buyer with deep expertise in
 Ads to join our dynamic team. If you're passionate about driving results through paid media and have a proven track record in scaling campaigns profitably, we want to hear from you!



Role Overview:

As our Media Buyer, you will play a crucial role in planning, executing, and optimizing
 Ads campaigns for our clients. You’ll work closely with our creative, data, and account management teams to develop paid media strategies that align with client goals and deliver measurable results. Your expertise will ensure high-performing campaigns that drive customer acquisition, increase ROI, and maximize brand growth.



Key Responsibilities:



Manage and execute
 Ads campaigns across various 
 brands, optimizing performance to meet and exceed KPIs.

Analyze campaign data and metrics to identify trends, provide actionable insights, and adjust strategies in real-time to improve performance.

Develop audience targeting strategies to reach specific customer segments and maximize ad reach and relevance.

Collaborate with the creative team to develop compelling ad creatives, copy, and landing pages that drive conversions.

Monitor budgets, report on campaign performance, and make recommendations for improvement based on data analysis.

Implement A/B testing to optimize ads, landing pages, and other campaign elements.

Stay up-to-date with the latest trends, tools, and best practices in paid social advertising to ensure we are leveraging the most effective strategies.

Work closely with clients to understand their business objectives, providing them with insights and suggestions to improve their media buying strategy.

Requirements:



Proven experience in media buying with a strong focus on
 Ads, managing budgets across multiple accounts.

Deep understanding of
 Ads Manager, audience targeting, campaign setup, and optimization techniques.

Strong analytical skills, with the ability to interpret campaign performance data and make data-driven decisions.

Familiarity with other paid media platforms (Google Ads, TikTok Ads) is a plus.

title: Data Extraction and Automation Specialist
description:
Company Overview



We are seeking a skilled Data Extraction and Automation Specialist who understands modern web scraping, lead data enrichment, automation workflows, and structured data management. Availability during U.S. Eastern hours is required.



Position Overview



We are hiring a Web Scraping and Lead Data Automation Specialist to support advanced list building, structured data extraction, and enrichment workflows. This role focuses on pulling targeted lead data at scale using Apify, Python, Clay, and APIs.



This is a hands on technical role for someone who understands scraping logic, selectors, pagination, anti-bot challenges, deduplication, and data normalization.



Key Responsibilities



• Build and maintain lead scraping workflows using Apify actors or Python

• Extract structured data from websites, directories, databases, and public sources

• Handle pagination, login flows, rate limits, and anti-scraping mechanisms

• Clean, dedupe, and normalize datasets

• Enrich scraped data using Clay

• Connect scraped and enriched data to CRM systems, Google Sheets, or CSV exports

• Build or integrate APIs for data transfer when needed

• Document scraping workflows and error handling processes

• Monitor automation health and troubleshoot broken workflows

• Deliver clean, structured lead lists ready for outbound campaigns



Qualifications



• Proven experience with web scraping using Apify or Python

• Strong understanding of HTML, CSS selectors, DOM structure

• Experience handling structured data formats such as JSON, CSV, and Excel

• Familiarity with REST APIs and authentication methods

• Experience using Clay for data enrichment

• Ability to troubleshoot scraping failures and adjust extraction logic

• Strong organizational skills and documentation habits

• Availability during U.S. Eastern hours



Preferred Skills



• Experience scraping education, healthcare, legal, or local business databases

• Familiarity with n8n, Zapier, or Make

• Experience integrating scraped data into Instantly, Apollo, Go High Level, or other CRMs

• Understanding of proxy management and anti-bot mitigation



Compensation



Pay is negotiable based on experience and technical depth.



Application Process



To apply for this position, candidates MUST complete all of the following steps:

 1. Submit a resume outlining web scraping and automation experience.

 2. Upload examples or screenshots of previous scraping projects or data extraction workflows.

 3. Submit a newly recorded video introduction where you:

• State the current date

• Explain your experience with web scraping, Apify, Python, and Clay

• Describe a scraping workflow you built and how you handled errors or blocked requests



Important Application Instructions



• Applications must be submitted through this link: 
/4130RpL

• Applications submitted directly on other platforms will not be reviewed

• All steps must be completed to be considered

title: Assistant Controller
description:
**PLEASE CAREFULLY READ ALL THE DETAILS BEFORE APPLYING***



JOB TITLE: Assistant Controller

Work Type: Remote/WFH, Full-time

Rate: TBD

Duration: This is for a long-term hire (3–5+ years minimum) to ensure stability and avoid turnover.

Working Hours : Central Time

Start Date: Target start date is early MAY



NOTE:



--> This role will begin as an Assistant Controller, with the opportunity for promotion to Controller if everything goes well.

--> This is an assistant role, not the main Controller position. The focus is more on providing support rather than leading or owning the full Controller function.

--> Client Interview Stage:

           There will be a live Excel skills test.

           There will be a business overview ? candidate reiterates to confirm comprehension.



JOB OVERVIEW:



The Controller will manage all fund, corporate, and property-level accounting and reporting across the client's real estate investment platform. This role oversees cash management, capital activity, investor operations, financial reporting, audit/tax support, and coordination with fund administrators and property managers. The ideal candidate is organized, detail-oriented, and comfortable operating in a fast-paced, multi-entity private equity environment.



JOB ROLE & RESPONSIBILITIES:



Treasury & Cash Management



Maintain LOC, draw, and cash activity trackers; process transfers, wires, and invoice payments.

Complete bank reconciliations, classify cash activity, and monitor liquidity.

Manage distributions, NACHA uploads in KeyBank, and quarterly cash-flow analysis.



Fund & Investor Accounting



Maintain investor records in Juniper Square; process sub docs, capital calls, rebalancing, and distributions.

Enter capital, GAV/NAV, and asset data into Juniper Square; create new entities as needed.

Prepare capital call amounts, distribution analysis, and quarterly reporting support.



Financial Reporting



Review monthly property financials and capital activity.

Prepare HEP Manager and selected SPV financials; compare actuals to forecasts.

Support quarterly fund reporting, PCAP reviews, and coordination with fund administrators.



Audit & Tax Support



Provide PBCs, schedules, valuation support, and respond to auditor requests.

Assist with annual tax workpapers, K-1 review/distribution, 1099 processing, and municipal/state filings.



Entity Administration



Maintain annual state filings, insurance renewals, and corporate compliance.

Manage intercompany billing, legal invoice approvals, and post-closing S&U/proration tracking.



Systems & Process Management



Work extensively in Juniper Square, QuickBooks, KeyBank, and Yardi.

Implement best practices with fund admins and improve internal workflows.



JOB REQUIREMENTS:



Qualifications:



Bachelor’s in Accounting or Finance; CPA preferred.

Ideally with 5–10+ years in real estate private equity, fund accounting, or property accounting. (can consider 3–4 years of experience, as long as you have the right experience)

Strong Excel and systems skills.

Excellent communication, organization, and deadline management.

Must have Private Equity Real Estate (PERE) experience.

Familiarity with complex fund structures.

Capable of and willing to commit to a long-term engagement (minimum of 3–5+ years) to ensure stability and minimize turnover.



Why Work with Gabtech Global, LLC?



At Gabtech Global, we are committed to supporting our consultants and creating an environment where people can grow and succeed.



We uphold our core values:



EXTRA MILE: We do more than what’s expected of us.

RELIABLE: We do what we say we’ll do. No follow-ups are needed.

TEAM PLAYER: We collaborate with each other to achieve our goals.

GENUINE CARE: We do what we do because we want to, not because we have to.

HUNGRY TO LEARN: We strive to be better every day.

title: Escalation Lead
description:
**PLEASE CAREFULLY READ ALL THE DETAILS BEFORE APPLYING***



Job Title: Escalation Lead

Work Type: Remote/WFH Fulltime 

Working Hours: M-F (40-45 hours) , plus on-call weekends

Start Date: in 2 weeks



JOB OVERVIEW:



The client’s  Escalation Lead is responsible for owning policy, risk, and scope decisions during high-impact client’s escalations. This role ensures that identity, access, and security-related incidents are resolved without introducing unnecessary security exposure, by validating root cause, defining safe remediation boundaries, and approving (or rejecting) configuration changes during live incidents.



This role represents the decision authority that currently exists informally in client’s escalations.



JOB ROLE & RESPONSIBILITIES: 



1. Conditional Access & Identity Policy Authority

Serve as the escalation authority for:

Conditional Access (CA) failures

Token issuance errors

Cloud PC / Windows App access scope questions

Interpret Entra ID sign-in logs and CA outcomes to determine why access was blocked.

Approve or deny:

CA exclusions

Access scope changes

Authentication flow adjustments

Prevent “blind” policy changes by enforcing root-cause validation first.



2. Security Alert Legitimacy & Incident Context

Validate security alerts from Defender and Threat Locker to determine:

True security incidents

False positives

Alerts tied to known remediation actions (e.g., decryption activity)

Confirm whether escalation requires:

Security response

Documentation only

No action

Act as the final authority on whether an alert is safe to disregard.



3. Escalation Decision Governance

Act as the policy gatekeeper during active escalations:

“Is this the correct fix?”

“Does this widen access beyond intent?”

Ensure remediation steps are:

Scoped

Intentional

Reversible

Require confirmation that a change resolves the issue before approving additional modifications.



4. Cross-Functional Technical Direction

Provide technical direction to:

Identity engineers

Security engineers

Infrastructure teams

Service desk leads

Guide troubleshooting steps (e.g., reviewing sign-in logs, validating access targets).

Escalate to senior engineers only when justified by evidence.



5. Escalation Flow Control

Control the decision phase of client’s escalation flow: Intake ? Validation ? Approved Change ? Confirmation ? Closure

Ensure escalation threads do not stall or expand without justification.

Clearly signal when a remediation path is approved or blocked.



6. Other responsibilities

Based on alert activity and volume, other responsibilities will be assigned

Process design and documentation

Flexibility - a key to success for this role



JOB REQUIREMENTS:



Technical Expertise

Deep knowledge of:

Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD)

Conditional Access policies

MFA / SSPR authentication flows

Cloud PC and Windows App access behavior



Strong ability to interpret:

Sign-in logs

Token issuance failures

Security alert context

Operational Judgment

Experience acting as a technical authority during live incidents

Ability to make risk-balanced decisions under time pressure

Comfortable blocking changes that increase risk, even when resolution is urgent

Communication

Clear, decisive communication in escalation threads and verbal communication

Ability to explain why a change is or is not approved

Confident interacting with senior engineers and leadership during incidents

Success Criteria

The role is successful when:

Escalations resolve without over-permissive policy changes

Identity and access issues are fixed with confirmed cause

Security alerts are correctly classified

Repeat escalations decrease due to better guardrails and documentation

Role Boundaries

Does not solely own day-to-day execution of fixes (that remains shared with the team)

Does own:

Approval of changes



Risk acceptance

Escalation direction

May oversee other resources working similar shifts/hours, acting as a Team Lead

title: Sales Team Lead - Remote Tech Startup (Performance Bonuses + Growth Path)
description:
We run a high-growth online platform that converts web traffic into paying customers using a mix of live sales reps and AI-assisted workflows. We're doing six figures monthly and scaling fast. We need a Sales Operations Manager to own the entire live sales function - the team, the systems, the numbers. You'll report directly to the founder.



You'll manage a team of sales reps who handle live customer conversations. Your job is to make them better, make the operation bigger, and make the numbers go up. Specifically:

- Hire, train, schedule, and hold reps accountable

- Own the full conversation-to-conversion funnel - first message to paid customer

- Analyze conversation logs, build scripts and playbooks, and push conversion rates up week over week

- Scale the team as traffic grows without letting conversion quality drop

- Track and report key metrics: conversion rate, revenue per rep, response times, conversations handled

- Build SOPs and training materials so new hires ramp fast

- Coordinate with the ads team on traffic volume, audience quality, and timing



You:

- 2+ years managing a sales or customer-facing team (not just individual contributor experience)

- You think in conversion rates, not vibes - decisions backed by data

- Strong written English

- You build systems and enforce them

- Comfortable with remote async tools

- Available for set working hours - this is a real full-time role, not freelance



Big Plus

- Experience with AI-assisted sales workflows or hybrid human/AI operations

- Track record of measurably improving conversion rates

- Experience managing remote teams across time zones



Compensation

- 78,500PHP/mo starting base (~$1,300 USD), adjusted for experience

- Performance bonuses tied to conversion targets and revenue milestones

- Direct access to the founder - your input shapes company strategy

- Clear growth path - as the operation scales, so does your role and comp



To Apply

Send a message with: who you are, your relevant experience, the largest team you've managed, and your resume

title: Operations Scheduling Supervisor (Remote | Full-Time)
description:
We're Hiring: Operations Scheduling Supervisor (Remote | Full-Time)



We are looking for an experienced Operations Scheduling Supervisor to join our team. This is a remote, full-time role suited for someone with a solid background in trades-based operations — think construction, plumbing, electrical, HVAC, or similar field service industries. You'll be the backbone of our scheduling function, keeping our technicians on track and our customers happy.



Key responsibilities:

Oversee and manage daily job scheduling across trades teams

Coordinate technician dispatch, job assignments, and route optimization

Monitor job progress and proactively resolve scheduling conflicts or delays

Handle incoming customer booking requests and schedule jobs efficiently

Conduct compliance checks, confirming job notes, photos, and completion details



Requirements:

Strongly preferred of at least 1 year of experience in an operations or scheduling role within a trades-based business (construction, plumbing, electrical, HVAC, landscaping, or similar)

Strong English written and verbal communication skills

Comfortable using time-tracking and productivity tools (for payroll purposes)

Reliable internet connection and a quiet, professional workspace



How to Apply?

Please apply directly to this job post and include:

1. Updated resume/CV (must be an accessible link)

2. Active
 address and WhatsApp number (for client communication)

3. A short introduction via: Loom video or Vocaroo voice recording. Share a bit about your experience related to the role. (Strongly preferred)

title: Junior Tax Assistant (Entry Level)
description:
About The CPA Dude: 

The CPA Dude, a fast-growing tax firm serving high-net-worth individuals and businesses with complex tax profiles. In this role, you will be the backbone of our payroll operations, ensuring smooth client management, document handling, and supporting the full cycle payroll process. As a critical member of our team, you'll help deliver tax transformations with comprehensive care, embodying our mission to become the Nike of tax firms.



Role

A junior tax assistant is an entry level role targeted towards new graduates with a few years of professional experience interested in breaking into the U.S tax preparation industry. Our firm offers continuous career growth for promising juniors willing to get their foot wet. 



Responsibilities

- Assist with data entry on US tax returns

- Coordinate with senior tax associates on data entry projects

- Oversee and prepare list of open items on behalf of senior tax associates

- Perform basic financial reconciliation tasks

- Maintain professionalism communicating requests and updates within internal team



Results

- Maintain a high level of client and team responsiveness (same day or within 24 hours)

- Ensure high client satisfaction and retention through exceptional client service

- Maintain accurate and complete client files for efficient tax preparation

- Ensure timely filing of all client tax documents and extensions

- Contribute to the firm's growth through process improvement and efficiency



Requirements

- Bachelor's degree in business or accounting 

OR

- Bachelor's degree with 3 years of professional accounting, bookkeeping, tax, or PHP CPA firm experience. 



Location

- Remote position with dedicated and secure workspace required, free of distractions.



Benefits

1. 100% remote work environment with the infrastructure to work from anywhere in the world

2. No BS corporate culture - we're modern, laid back, and straight to the point

3. Professional development opportunities and continuous learning

4. Collaborative team culture with awesome co-workers

5. Opportunity to grow with a fast-expanding firm

6. Digital-first approach with cutting-edge tax technology

7. Work alongside tax professionals focused on innovative tax strategies

8. Entrepreneurial environment that rewards initiative and fresh ideas



Core Values

1. Communication: Honest, kind candor with prompt responses. We value over-communication—more context is always better.

2. Ownership: Taking full responsibility for work and relationships. Deadlines are non-negotiable, and quality work is expected.

3. Work Smarter, Not Harder: Leveraging better technology, refining processes, and bringing fresh, creative thinking.



Software You'll Use:

ProConnect (Intuit) – tax return and extension prep

TaxDome – CRM and secure client communication

QuickBooks Online – for coordinating with bookkeepers

Google Workspace – Docs, Sheets, Drive, Gmail (we don't use Microsoft Office)



Working Hours

Regular hours: 8 Hours with 6 hours overlap in US time

Weekend hours during tax season may be flexible.



Hiring Process

Apply and record a 
/Video introducing yourself and explaining why you are a good fit for this position.

 that and your resume to  : 



Prescreen interview (30 minutes)

Technical test (take-home)

Panel/culture interview (60 minutes)

title: Outbound Sales Account Manager - Construction Materials| Family Business 5 Years+
description:
Remote – Philippines | Full-Time | Long-Term

 $4.5-$8.5 /hour (based on experience) + Annual team bonuses TBD



About us



We are a fast-growing construction materials supplier working with builders, contractors, and developers nationwide.



We invest heavily in training, systems, and people - and we expect performance, loyalty and integrity in return.



We are a family-run business with a strong, loyal offshore team. You will be joining 6 experienced Filipino tea
mbers, many of whom have been with us for over 4 years.



The Role (What You’ll Actually Do) -  This is a sales-driven outbound account management role.  You will be speaking daily with existing customers (warm leads) who have already bought from us.



Your job is to:

- Rebuild EXISTING CUSTOMER relationships

- Generate repeat business from existing customers (HOT) - not cold leads

- Increase order value by being smart understanding requirments and upsell accordingly 

- Use the CRM and you productivity will be judged on output - 30-60 calls a day

- Build Quotes, get payments and load orders and monitor entire delivery workflow for your clients. 



Move customers up tiers (e.g. Silver ? Gold)

1. Outbound Sales & Account Management (Core Focus)

Call existing customers daily

Re-engage inactive buyers

Build relationships with UK builders and contractors

Ask structured questions about:

Current projects

What they are buying

Where they are currently purchasing

You are expected to sell, not just check in.

Log everything in the CRM and work the CRM to keep the sales flow



2. Sales Skills & Negotiation

Handle pricing conversations confidently

Position us against competitors (Jewson, Selco, B&Q, online suppliers)

Negotiate professionally to win business

Identify upsell and cross-sell opportunities

You must be comfortable:

Challenging customers

Handling objections

Driving decisions



3. CRM & Workflow (Pipedrive)

Work daily inside Pipedrive CRM (this is your core system)

Log all calls, notes, and deal updates

Manage your pipeline accurately

Use our AI-enhanced tools to support your sales activity

You must be comfortable working across:

Multiple tabs/screens

High call volumes

Structured workflows



4. Training & Sales Playbooks

You will be partly trained using:

Internal sales playbooks

Call scripts and frameworks

Product and construction material guides

You are not expected to know everything on day one - but you are expected to learn fast and apply immediately, think on the fly and do the role i specified or you will be out. 



What We’re Looking For (Non-Negotiable)

Sales Experience (Required)

Outbound or inbound sales experience

Comfortable on calls (this is a phone-heavy role)

Experience handling objections and closing

Construction experience is a bonus, not required.

Communication & Confidence

Excellent / Strong spoken English

Confident speaking to UK / western customers

Clear, structured communication

Not overly passive or scripted

Mindset & Work Style - PROACTIVE NOT REACTIVE - Proactive (you take action without being told)

Competitive and results-driven

Organised and disciplined

Comfortable working in a structured system

Team-oriented (this is not a solo role) - We work as a unit

Working Setup screens & Expectations

Fixed hourly wage via Hubstaff (time-tracked)

Full-time, long-term role

3-month probation period



Must have:

Stable internet

Quiet working environment

Multiple screens (strongly preferred)

Stable home life - no family dramas or exposures that could affect our business please. We have had enough experience of this  I have been recruiting from PH for 15 years. 



Performance-based bonuses (team commission – team-focused model - No solo players here)... We have loyal PH tea
mbers appraoching 5 years with us who will be eligble for paid health insurance by us. 



To set expectations clearly:

We have licensed sales engineers in our team earning ~$6.15 /hour who are very happy as we are move than just a VA role to:... The world is changing fast with the war, energy, AI taking jobs and they appreciate stability and growth with us. 



- Advanced training

- Stability

- Strong team culture

- Long-term growth

- We are a family business:



If you perform and support the team, we will support you We run company trips and value long-term relationships... We have a team of 6 in Philapines already and will be expanding. We have unfortunately let go multiple people also as they fail probation or get lazy and let us and themselves down. Dont be this person....



Open Positions

We are hiring 3–5 tea
mbers for this role.



Training & Support

Direct support from UK-based sales managers

Ongoing coaching and feedback

Proven systems and workflows 

All the tools and leads you need to be comfortable



How to Apply (MANDATORY REQUIREMENTS)

You must submit ALL of the following: and fill in the below form 



HERE--->>   



Your CV

A short written answer:

“Describe your sales experience and how you would handle a customer who says your price is too high.”

A short video (REQUIRED – no video = no review):

Please record a 1–2 minute video introducing yourself and answering:



Your sales experience

Why you are a good fit for this role

How comfortable you are speaking to UK customers

Applications without a video will not be considered.

Any relevant experience 



Final Note

This is not a passive role.If you are:

Comfortable on calls

Confident in sales

Willing to learn and improve

Humble and loyal 

Have a family ethic 

AI smart and commercially aware

Dont apply if your profile / experience doesnt match or something not relevant please - We wish you well but we need what we need. 



This is a long-term opportunity to grow with a serious business.... Time wasters not wanted 



Thank you for reading this and taking interest - we look forward to interviewing you and welcome you to the CW team. 



We wish you and your families safety, prosperity and a bright future!  

CW Recruitment Team

title: Community Operations Specilist (Customer Service)
description:
Video Introduction Required to Apply



To be considered for this position, all applicants must submit a short video introduction.



In your video, please share:

• A brief overview of who you are and your experience

• Your level of proficiency with Go High Level

• How you would add value to our community and support our members



Please note: Applications without a video introduction will not be considered.



We are a rapidly growing Digital Product Creators company focused on helping entrepreneurs turn their knowledge, skills, and ideas into profitable digital products using our all-in-one system powered by Go High Level.



As we continue to scale, we are looking for a Community Operations Specialist who is both professional and personable—someone who can confidently support, train, and guide our members while contributing to a high-performance, results-driven environment.



Role Overview

In this role, you will serve as a key point of contact within our community. You will support new and existing members by providing guidance, answering questions, and helping them successfully implement our systems to launch and grow their digital products.



This position requires someone who is not only knowledgeable, but also comfortable leading trainings and clearly teaching Go High Level in a way that is easy for others to understand and apply.



Key Responsibilities

• Onboard new members and ensure a smooth, professional experience

• Provide timely and effective customer support

• Host live trainings, onboarding sessions, and Q&A calls

• Train members on Go High Level and system implementation

• Guide members step-by-step as they build and launch their products

• Monitor progress, increase engagement, and support retention efforts

• Conduct outreach and follow-ups to keep members on track

• Contribute to a positive, supportive, and results-focused community



Additional Responsibilities

• Assist with certification and training programs

• Lead workshops or step in as a trainer when needed

• Support revenue growth by recommending relevant products and services

• Help members fully utilize the platform to achieve their goals



Ideal Candidate

We are seeking someone who is:

• Proficient in Go High Level (required)

• Experienced in training, coaching, or leading groups

• Professional, organized, and proactive

• Comfortable communicating clearly in both group and one-on-one settings

• Tech-savvy and able to simplify complex systems

• Passionate about helping others succeed



This is an excellent opportunity to be part of a company that is growing quickly while making a meaningful impact on the success of our members.  If you are confident in your ability to support, train, and lead within a dynamic environment, we look forward to reviewing your application.



Submit your video introduction to get started.

title: Remote Personal Assistant + Content Creator (Travel, Family & Property)
description:

I have a lot of raw content and a strong lifestyle to document. I’m looking for someone who can help turn this into engaging content while also supporting with day-to-day organisation and operations.



?



???? Role Overview

 • Content creation (TikTok & Instagram)

 • Personal assistant, lifestyle, and property support



This can grow into a full-time role for the right person.



You must be comfortable switching between creative work and structured organisation.



???? While the workload is balanced, strong content creation skills are essential.



?



???? Content Creation (Key Skill Required)



You must be confident in:

 • Editing TikTok and Instagram videos from raw clips

 • Understanding what performs well (hooks, pacing, storytelling)

 • Following trends and applying them in a natural, lifestyle-focused way

 • Creating and maintaining a content calendar (weekly/monthly themes)

 • Researching similar accounts and analysing what works

 • Posting consistently and engaging with other accounts

 • Helping refine and improve the content direction over time



?



???? Personal & Lifestyle Support

 • Book flights, hotels, and transfers

 • Set flight alerts and track pricing

 • Research family-friendly activities in different locations

 • Book restaurants in different locations

 • Organise children’s schedules and appointments

 • Book medical, beauty, and general appointments

 • Manage a shared family calendar

 • Provide a daily summary of tasks requiring approval or payment



?



???? Property & Operations

 • Manage Airbnb inbox and guest communication

 • Monitor bookings and follow up with guests

 • Help optimise pricing and send offers

 • Assist in setting up listings on 
 and VRBO

 • Sync and manage calendars across platforms

 • Coordinate cleaners, laundry, and property services

 • Book guest transfers when needed



?



???? Financial & Admin

 • Track expenses across properties

 • Review bank statements and break down costs monthly

 • Monitor rent payments

 • Set reminders for bills and deadlines

 • Prepare summaries for accountants

 • Create spreadsheets (flights, bookings, costs, luggage, etc.)



?



???? Communication

 • Strong English required (Spanish a bonus — translator tools are fine)

 • Comfortable communicating via WhatsApp and

 • Comfortable in group chats and coordinating with multiple people

 • Proactive with follow-ups and updates



?



???? What I’m Looking For

 • Strong TikTok / Reels editing skills (essential)

 • Highly organised and detail-oriented

 • Proactive and able to think independently

 • Fast, responsive, and reliable

 • Comfortable balancing creative and admin tasks



?



???? Requirements

 • Experience editing short-form content (CapCut or similar)

 • Experience as a VA or assistant is a bonus

 • Comfortable with Google Sheets, calendars, booking platforms

title: Sales Associate that close sales
description:
**TO APPLY YOU MUST CREATE A SHORT VIDEO INTRODUCING YOURSELF AND WHY YOU WILL BE THE PEFERCT CANIDADTE FOR THIS POSITION.



Sales Associate – Digital Product Training (75+ Calls Daily)



Position: Full-Time Sales Closer

Industry: Digital Products / Online Education

Location: Remote

Compensation: Base + Commission (High Earning Potential)



About Us



We are a fast-growing digital education company that helps entrepreneurs create and sell digital products. We host live trainings and sell an all-in-one Digital Products Creators Sales & Marketing System.



We are NOT looking for someone who “likes sales.”

We are looking for someone who lives and breathes closing.



Your Role: You will be responsible for calling warm leads daily from paid ads and a free training we conducted to:



* Sell tickets to our Digital Product Creators Mentorship Training

* Close sales for our Digital Product Creators Sales & Marketing System

* Follow up with prospects consistently

* Overcome objections confidently

* Hit daily call and revenue targets



You will be on the phone ALL DAY. This is a performance-driven role.



Daily Requirements



* Minimum 75-100 outbound calls per day

* Consistent follow-up via phone & SMS

* Daily reporting of:



Calls made

Conversations

Sales closed

Revenue generated

Comfortable handling objections and pushing for the close



Who We’re Looking For



You MUST have:



* Minimum **2+ years of phone sales experience**

* Strong closing skills

* Aggressive, confident, and results-driven personality

* Thick skin (you will hear “no” often)

* Ability to build urgency and create excitement

* High energy and professional tone on calls

* Reliable internet and quiet workspace



This is NOT for beginners.

This is for closers who want to win.



Compensation



* Competitive base pay

* Commission on every sale

* Performance bonuses for hitting targets

* High income potential for top performers



If you cannot handle rejection, high call volume, or aggressive targets — this role is not for you.



???? To Apply



Send:



1. A short video (2–3 minutes) introducing yourself and why you will be the perfect canidate for this position

2. Your sales experience (include metrics if possible)

3. Your average daily call volume in past roles

4. Why you believe you are a closer and not just a salesperson



Only serious closers apply.

title: Cold Caller / Appointment Setter (Merchant Services)
description:
???? About the Role



You will call business owners in Las Vegas. (restaurants, auto shops, retail, etc.) and set qualified appointments for our sales team.



You must be comfortable:



Getting past gatekeepers

Controlling the conversation

Handling objections in real-time

Booking high-quality appointments (not fluff)

???? Responsibilities

Make 150–250 outbound calls per day

Call during Las Vegas business hours (PST)

Speak directly with owners/decision-makers

Deliver a strong, natural pitch (not scripted)

Overcome objections confidently

Book qualified appointments

Log all activity in CRM

? REQUIREMENTS (STRICT)

2+ years B2B cold calling experience (U.S. market REQUIRED)

Must have worked night shift / U.S. hours before

Clear, confident English (minimal accent)

Proven ability to set appointments consistently

Strong objection-handling skills

Reliable internet + quiet work setup

Professional headset

???? BIG ADVANTAGE IF YOU HAVE:

Merchant services / payment processing experience

Experience calling:

Restaurants

Auto repair shops

Local service businesses

Familiarity with dialers + CRM systems

Track record hitting daily/weekly KPIs

???? Compensation

$6 hour (based on experience)

$50–$150 per closed deal

Weekly pay via Remitly

Performance-based raises

???? KPIs

150–250 calls/day

5–10+ qualified appointments/week

High show-up rate on booked calls

???? Why This Role Is Different

You’re calling into a strong, easy-to-explain offer

High earning potential through bonuses

Long-term role with growth opportunities

Direct impact on company revenue

???? HOW TO APPLY (MANDATORY)



Only serious applicants will be considered.



Send:



Voice recording (30–60 seconds)

Your B2B cold calling experience (U.S. clients)

Industries you’ve called

Average daily call volume

Confirmation you can work Las Vegas hours (PST)

Internet speed test screenshot

???? DO NOT APPLY IF:

No B2B cold calling experience

No U.S. calling experience

Cannot work night shift (Philippines)

Only customer service/inbound background

title: Facebook Ad Specialist - Home Renovation Services: Retainer + Large Performance Bonuses!
description:
HB Windows & Doors Inc. is a premium, family-run window and door installation company serving the Greater Toronto Area. We're hiring a
 Ads Specialist who can take ownership of our lead generation strategy with urgency and creativity.



We offer one of the most unique product bundles in the Canadian window industry — exclusive access to triple-pane + laminated glass. Your job will be to bring this offer to life, generate leads, and help us fill our calendar.



Compensation:

- Recommended Retainer: $300–$500 CAD/month (based on skills and results)

- Bonus Per Booked Appointment: $20–$60 CAD



*** THIS IS A PERFORMANCE-DRIVEN ROLE WITH SIGNIFICANT BONUS POTENTIAL BASED ON RESULTS ***



Responsibilities:

- Build, manage, and optimize
 ad campaigns for lead volume

- Test and refine messaging for our exclusive laminated + triple-pane offer

- Work with our Graphic Designer and VA to execute creatives and follow-ups

- Drive Cost Per Lead (CPL) down and maintain high close potential

- Report weekly on lead volume, CPL, CTR, and cost-per-booked-appointment



Requirements:

- Proven success running
 Ads for local or home services

- Experience with $1K–$3K ad budgets

- Strong copywriting and funnel building mindset

- Familiarity with lead form campaigns

- Ability to build and launch landing pages (design not required!)





How to Apply:

To be considered, follow these instructions carefully:



Use the subject line: HBWD - ROCKSTAR AD MANAGER



Answer the following questions:



1. What types of local service businesses have you run successful
 campaigns for?



2. How do you ensure lead quality while maximizing volume?



3. What’s your typical process in Month 1 of a new account?



4. What do you think is the biggest mistake home service companies make in
 Ads?



Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be reviewed.

title: COO / Operations Lead (Dropshipping Brand)
description:
We are hiring a COO / Operations Lead (Dropshipping Brand) to handle product launches, store management, and team coordination.

Salary: $800/month

, Asana, ClickUp, Trello, etc.)

title: Bookkeeper  (E-commerce | Full-Time | Remote)
description:
About Us



We are a fast-growing 7-figure e-commerce dropshipping company, primarily operating on Shopify. We work with multiple suppliers and vendors, managing high daily order volumes and transactions across different systems.Our business is fast-paced, detail-driven, and systems-focused. We value accuracy, consistency, and people who take ownership of their work.



The Role



We are looking for a reliable and detail-oriented  Bookkeeper to support our day-to-day financial operations.

This is a hands-on, execution-focused role where your primary responsibility will be to ensure all financial transactions are accurately recorded, tracked, and reconciled. 



Key Responsibilities

1. Accounts Payable & Receivable

Process and track vendor payments via bank and dealer portals

Ensure all payments are completed accurately and on time

Monitor incoming payments from customers (wire transfers)

Maintain clear records of all receivables and payables



2. Reconciliation & Daily Financial Checks

Reconcile Shopify payouts

Identify and flag any discrepancies or missing payments



3. Vendor & Supplier Management

Process and track supplier credits and refunds

Reconcile vendor statements and maintain accurate credit balances

Follow up on missing credits or discrepancies when needed



4. Bookkeeping & Data Entry

Input Cost of Goods Sold (COGS)



Requirements

2–3 minimum years experience in bookkeeping or finance support

Experience with e-commerce businesses (Shopify preferred)

Familiarity with accounting tools like Xero, QuickBooks, or similar

Strong attention to detail and high level of accuracy

Comfortable working with spreadsheets (Google Sheets / Excel)

Good written and verbal English communication





What We’re Looking For

Highly detail-oriented — you catch small errors others miss

Organized and consistent in your work

Reliable and able to follow processes daily

Proactive in flagging issues or discrepancies

Comfortable working in a structured, fast-moving environment



What’s in it for You?

Fully remote, stable full-time role

 Opportunity to grow within a scaling e-commerce business

Learn and develop within a systems-driven environment

Work with an international team



Working Hours: Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM EST (8 hours/day)

Salary: $600/month

10 days Paid Time Off 

Monthly Team Bonuses 

Individual Performance Bonuses 



If this sounds like a good fit, we’d love to hear from you. Apply here:

title: ***AMAZING Shopify SHRINE Theme - Product Listing - Needed ASAP***
description:

If you’re detail-oriented, highly organized, and experienced with Shopify’s theme editor and product management tools, we want to hear from you!



Responsibilities:

Set up and optimize product pages using the Shrine theme’s section-based editor to ensure a clean, high-converting shopping experience.



Collaborate with the marketing and design teams to create visually appealing product listings and organize products into relevant collections.



Manage inventory visibility and ensure all products are live and properly displayed in the store.



Perform regular updates to collections, homepage banners, and featured products, making full use of the Shrine theme’s capabilities.



Troubleshoot layout, formatting, or theme-related issues and implement effective fixes.



Install and manage Shopify apps and integrations to enhance store performance and customer experience.



Assist with light product image editing using tools like Canva or Photoshop to ensure consistency and visual appeal.



Stay up-to-date with the latest Shopify features, tools, and best practices related to the Shrine theme.



Requirements:

Strong understanding of Shopify’s product setup, collections, metafields, and theme editor.



Fluent in English with excellent written and verbal communication skills.



High attention to detail and commitment to delivering error-free, polished product pages.



Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.



Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities.



Preferred Qualifications:



Experience in the e-commerce industry, especially with Direct-to-Consumer (DTC) brands.



Familiarity with apps like OneClick OCU, Klaviyo, and other Shopify-compatible tools.



Basic understanding of SEO. 



Experience using Google Analytics or Shopify Analytics to identify optimisation opportunities.



We are looking forward to your application! 



We appreciate you taking the time to complete this application. 





Best Wishes,

Mert Yan

title: Digital Marketing & E-commerce Assistant at True Gear
description:
At True Gear, we’re a small but dedicated team based in Australia, and we're on a mission to provide top-notch portable power solutions and adventure equipment for people who love caravanning. We’re looking for a new tea
mber who is ready to jump in and help us grow. Could it be you?



About the Team:



Our team is currently made up of two awesome people: myself, the business owner, and one full-time tea
mber based in the Philippines. We work closely together, with lots of collaboration, and I’m involved in the day-to-day operations. Now, we're looking to bring someone else on board to help us expand our marketing efforts and take True Gear to the next level.



The Role:



This position is all about analysing and improving our marketing, learning on the job, and helping to drive growth through key platforms. The role will start with a paid trial period of about a month, during which we’ll get to know each other and see if we’re a good fit. If things go well, the role will evolve into a more structured position that fits your schedule.



You’ll be working across six key platforms (from most to least important):



Google Ads

Shopify

Google Sheets

Klaviyo 
 Marketing)

Meta Ads 
 & Instagram)

Canva





While the role has a clear structure, we’re a small team, so you'll need to be adaptable and ready to take on new tasks and challenges that come up (you'll never be left alone if you're unsure so don't stress).



What You’ll Be Doing:



Google Ads: You'll start by analyzing product profitability and desirability using templates we provide, and then manage our Google Ads campaigns. This includes keyword research and using a three-tier Google Ads shopping approach.



Meta Ads & Klaviyo: Once you’re comfortable with Google Ads, you’ll take on responsibilities with Meta Ads 
 & Instagram) and help set up
 flows in Klaviyo.



Collaboration: You'll be working closely with the team, providing updates and collaborating on strategies to drive sales and awareness for True Gear. Your contributions will have a direct impact on our growth.



What We Need from You:



Google Ads Experience: We’re looking for someone who is comfortable with Google Ads, especially keyword targeting and managing shopping campaigns. Familiarity with the three-tier shopping strategy is a big plus.



Shopify Experience: While not essential, experience with Shopify will make your life a lot easier. If you're new to it, no worries -we’ll help you get up to speed.



Meta Ads, Klaviyo, Canva: If you have experience with these tools, awesome! If not, don’t worry. We’ll provide training to help you learn.



Flexible and Self-Motivated: As part of a small team, it’s important that you can take ownership of your work.



Communication Skills: Since we’re a remote team with flexible working hours, good communication is key. We need someone who is proactive about updating the team, asking questions when needed, and keeping everyone in the loop.



What We Offer:



A Friendly, Supportive Environment: We’re a tight-knit team where your contribution will truly make a difference. You won’t just be a cog in the machine - you can really move the needle.



Flexibility: After the trial period, we’re happy to accommodate a schedule that works for you. We can discuss full-time hours and can make it work as long as commitments are met and communication stays clear.



Training and Growth: We’ll support your learning and development, especially with platforms like Meta Ads, Klaviyo, and Canva. This is a role that will allow you to expand your marketing skills while helping us grow the business.



Work-Life Balance: We understand the importance of balancing work with your personal life, and we offer flexibility to make that happen.



Expectations:



We value commitment, responsibility, and open communication. During the trial period, we’ll be assessing how well you follow through on tasks and communicate when challenges arise. If you’re someone who thrives in a small, fast-paced environment and can take ownership of your work, we think you’ll love it here.



Application Process:



Application Deadline: April 17th, 5pm AEST

How to Apply: If this role sounds like a good fit, we’d love to hear from you. Click the application link below to submit your application.



Application link:

title: Marketing & Operations Associate – Second in Command
description:
About Rig53

Social network for professional truck drivers. Closed beta. Small team. You will be the founder's right hand.



What This Role Is

You are not a task-doer. You are the second person in the company.



You bring marketing skills (influencer outreach, lead generation, social media). You also handle operations and administration so I can focus on product and partnerships.



You will help me figure out what to do, not just do what I say.



What You'll Do

Marketing (you lead this)

Influencer outreach – find, contact, track trucker influencers

Lead generation – recruit beta testers from
, Reddit, forums

Social media management – schedule posts, engage, help grow audience

Make recommendations – what's working, what's not, what we should try



Operations & Administration (you handle this)

Manage my calendar and

Track tasks in ClickUp

Maintain CRM (HubSpot)

Document processes as we build them



Strategy (we do this together)

Weekly planning – what are our priorities?

Problem-solving – how do we get more users?

Feedback – tell me when I'm wrong or missing something



Who You Are

You have experience with influencer marketing, lead generation, or social media management (2+ years or proven results)

You are organized – you use tools like CRMs and project management software without being taught

You are proactive – you see what needs to be done and do it

You want to grow with a company, not collect a paycheck

You are comfortable with ambiguity – startups change daily

You speak and write English naturally (not stiff/corporate)



What You Get

Direct access to the founder – no layers

Real ownership – your ideas will become reality

Growth path – VA ? Marketing Lead ? Chief of Staff (based on company growth)

Equity discussion at 6 months for strong performers

A chance to build something from the ground up



Hiring Process

Apply – Send the items below

Screening – 3 questions via chat

Video/phone call – 20 minutes

Paid trial ($10-$15 for 2–3 hours of real work)

Offer



To Apply

Subject line: RIG53 #2 – [Your Name]

Send:

A short cover letter explaining:

What marketing work you've done (influencer, lead gen, social)

What tools you use (CRM, project management, scheduling)

Why you want to be someone's second-in-command at a startup

One example of a marketing result you drove (numbers if possible)

One question about Rig53 or the founder

title: GoHighLevel CRM Builder — Real Estate and Mortgage Advisor System
description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

I am a real estate broker and mortgage advisor in Chicago. I need a skilled GoHighLevel (GHL) VA to build a complete CRM system from scratch. Everything is fully documented — you are building from written instructions, not creating strategy.



This is a project-based engagement estimated at 130–180 hours over 4 weeks. If the build is clean and you work well independently, there is ongoing maintenance work available after the project.



WHAT YOU WILL BUILD

— 18 sales pipelines with exact stage names (all documented)

— 60+ tag library entered exactly as specified

— 50+
 and SMS sequences (all copy written word for word — you enter it)

— Full workflow automation map — tags trigger sequences automatically

— ManyChat integration: 5 lead qualification flows with complete scripts

— GHL AI bot: 3 workflows (after-hours, missed call, 45-minute gap)

— BombBomb video tool connected to GHL

— All action-based automation rules (reply, book, close, bounce, DNC)



EVERYTHING IS PROVIDED

You will receive complete PDF documentation for every component of this system. Every
 is written. Every sequence is mapped. Every tag is named. Your job is accurate entry and configuration — not creative work.



HOW THE BUILD WORKS

Phase 1 (Days 1–5): Foundation — pipelines, tags, calendar, phone, signature

Phase 2 (Days 6–18): All sequences and automation triggers

Phase 3 (Days 19–26): ManyChat, GHL bot, BombBomb, integrations



I verify the work at the end of each phase before you proceed. Nothing moves forward until the current phase is confirmed correct.



REQUIREMENTS

— Proven GoHighLevel experience — you must be able to show me a GHL sub-account you have built

— Ability to follow written documentation exactly without improvising

— Strong attention to detail — one wrong tag name breaks an automation

— Available for a daily 10-minute check-in during the build (US Central Time morning preferred)

— Fluent written English for communication



NICE TO HAVE

— ManyChat experience

— Real estate or mortgage VA background



RATE

$8–$15/hour depending on experience. Estimated 130–180 hours total.



TO APPLY

Please send: (1) A screenshot or short video of a GHL sub-account you have built, (2) Your answer to this question: What is the difference between a GHL workflow trigger and a pipeline stage action? Applications without both will not be reviewed.

title: SEO Content Specialist (Blog Writer & On-Page SEO Manager)
description:
Role Overview



We’re looking for a detail-oriented SEO Content Specialist to support our growing client portfolio at Warren Digital. This role focuses on publishing and optimising blog content for SEO — not creating strategy.



You’ll be responsible for executing clearly defined SEO tasks to improve rankings, internal linking, and overall on-page performance across client sites.



Note: This is an execution-focused role. No strategy creation is required.



Key Responsibilities

Content Publishing & Optimisation

Upload and publish blog content across client websites (primarily WordPress or similar CMS)

Format content for readability (headings, spacing, lists, etc.)

Add and optimise images (including generating AI images when required)

Ensure all blogs meet SEO best practices before publishing

On-Page SEO Execution

Write and optimise:

Meta titles & descriptions

H1–H3 structure

Image alt text

URL slugs

Implement schema markup (Article, FAQ, etc.)

Apply E-E-A-T principles (Experience, Expertise, Authority, Trust)

Ensure proper keyword placement and density

Internal Linking

Add relevant internal links across blogs and service pages

Identify linking opportunities to improve site structure and SEO authority

Content Enhancement

Improve existing blog posts with:

Updated formatting

Better keyword targeting

Additional internal links

Image enhancements

EEAT improvements (author signals, credibility, trust elements)

Requirements

Proven experience in SEO content publishing

Strong understanding of:

On-page SEO

Meta tags & schema markup

Internal linking strategies

E-E-A-T principles

Experience with CMS platforms (WordPress preferred)

Ability to generate or source relevant images (AI tools like Midjourney, DALL·E, etc. are a plus)

High attention to detail and consistency

Nice to Have

Experience with SurferSEO, Ahrefs, SEMrush, or similar tools

Basic understanding of technical SEO

Copywriting skills for conversion-focused content

Experience developing AI prompts for content and meta tag generation

Compensation

$5 USD per hour

Ongoing work for the right candidate

Application Requirements



To be considered, please submit:



1. 3-Minute Loom Video



Explain:



Your experience with SEO content publishing

Your process for optimising a blog post

Tools you use

2. Practical Test Task



Visit: 



Provide a short audit outlining:



5 specific things you would do to improve the site’s SEO, for example:



Content improvements

Internal linking opportunities

On-page SEO fixes

Technical or UX suggestions

Blog/content strategy ideas



(Be specific — we’re looking for actionable insights, not generic advice.)

title: Senior Frontend Engineer
description:
We are hiring a senior frontend engineer to build and own high-quality product experiences across our

web application. This role is for someone who is strong in React and TypeScript, partners closely with

UX and product, turns Figma designs into polished production UI, and uses AI tools pragmatically to

move faster on styling, testing, automation, and frontend functionality.



What You Will Do

? Build and maintain complex frontend product flows in React and TypeScript.

? Partner closely with UX and design to translate Figma into clean, responsive, production-ready

interfaces.

? Improve product quality through better component architecture, frontend testing, and UI

consistency.

? Use AI-assisted tools to accelerate implementation, styling iteration, test generation, QA workflows,

and repetitive frontend tasks without lowering engineering standards.

? Own frontend functionality end to end, including state management, async data flows, error

handling, edge cases, and performance.

? Build and maintain reusable UI patterns, components, and design system conventions.

? Write and maintain automated tests for critical user flows, visual regressions, and frontend behavior.

? Collaborate with product, backend, and design to ship quickly and make sound technical tradeoffs.

similar.

automation.

maintainable component styling.

title: Audit - SEO, Google Ads and website setup
description:
We are a natural skincare company. Our products are award-winning and trusted by thousands of families. Recently, we’ve experienced a drop in organic performance and ROI on google ads — and we’re looking for an experienced SEO consultant to audit our website, identify the causes, and create a clear recovery plan.

Role Overview

You’ll lead a full SEO and search audit to help us understand why organic revenue has declined and how to rebuild sustainable performance. Focus areas include technical SEO, domain consolidation, paid search cannibalization, and improving our visibility in both Google and emerging AI-driven search results.

Key Responsibilities

• Perform a comprehensive SEO audit (technical, content, and on-page).

• Identify domain conflicts and duplicate indexing between 
, .com, and Shopify URLs.

• Check for hreflang configuration issues and recommend fixes.

• Analyse Google Ads overlap and develop strategies to reduce organic cannibalization.

• Audit meta titles and descriptions for CTR improvement opportunities.

• Review data in GA4 and Google Search Console for accuracy and insights.

• Suggest practical, high-impact changes to restore organic rankings and traffic.

• Provide clear documentation and communicate findings via report or video walkthrough.

Qualifications

• 4+ years of strong SEO experience, particularly in eCommerce or Shopify.

• Proven success recovering or improving organic traffic for online stores.

• Expertise with Google Search Console, GA4, Ahrefs or SEMrush, and technical SEO tools.

• Understanding of cannibalisation, hreflang, and structured data best practices.

• Excellent communication and analytical reporting skills.

• Experience optimizing for user engagement and conversion, not just rankings.

Deliverables

• Full SEO Audit Report with findings and actionable recommendations.

• List of priority technical fixes.

• Updated meta title and meta description suggestions for key pages.

• Documentation or SOPs for maintaining healthy search performance long-term.

title: AI Engineer
description:
About the Role



We are looking for an AI Engineer to join a small, fast-moving team building an AI-powered SaaS platform from the ground up. You will own the intelligence layer of the platform - the components responsible for language understanding, semantic search, embedding pipelines, and speech-to-text processing.



This is a full-time position.



What You Will Be Doing



- Building and maintaining a provider-agnostic AI abstraction layer supporting multiple LLM providers

- Designing and managing embedding generation pipelines with structured metadata filtering

- Integrating and maintaining a vector database for semantic search at scale

- Building and maintaining a speech-to-text transcription pipeline with quality validation and fallback handling

- Implementing semantic analysis logic to extract structured insights from conversational text

- Working from detailed written specifications provided by the product owner

- Submitting daily written progress updates





Must-Have Skills



- Python - 3+ years in production AI or ML systems

- OpenAI API - embeddings and chat completions in production (not just tutorials)

- Vector database experience - Weaviate, Qdrant, Pinecone, or equivalent in a production environment

- Embedding pipeline design - you understand embedding drift, dimensionality, and metadata filtering

- Speech-to-text integration - Whisper or equivalent in a production pipeline

- NLP or text analysis in deployed, production systems

- Building AI abstraction layers that are not hard-coded to a single provider





Nice to Have



- Experience with behavior or intent detection from conversational text

- Familiarity with LangChain, LlamaIndex, or similar orchestration frameworks

- Background in conversation intelligence, speech analytics, or call center technology

- Experience with Weaviate specifically including hybrid search

- Understanding of RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) architectures in production





Non-Negotiables



- Production systems only. Tutorial projects and demos do not qualify.

- You must design systems that can swap AI providers without rewriting core application logic. Hard-coding a single provider is a disqualifier.

- You must understand that embedding generation and vector storage are separate responsibilities.

- You must think about fallback handling, rate limits, and model output validation - not just the happy path.

- IP assignment agreement and NDA must be signed before engagement begins.

- You will not communicate directly with other developers. All coordination goes through the product owner.





Engagement Terms



- Competitive rate based on market levels for your region

- Contract with IP assignment and NDA





How to Apply



Send the following:



1. A description of a production AI system you built - what it did, what the hard parts were, and what you would do differently today

2. Your GitHub profile or a code sample showing Python AI integration work

3. Your expected monthly rate and availability



Shortlisted candidates will be required to take a technical skills knowledge test.
